data.description:
## Why

Outbound link sync broke because production routes outbound updates through the **batch** applier (`batch_dispatch.update_one` + `_mutation_to_batch_dict`), while the link/comment/label features and their tests lived in the **typed-leaf** applier `apply_outbound._apply_outbound_update` — which production does not execute. Capabilities had to be re-added to the batch path one bug at a time (parent 8b25, comments 87e4, links 3f04). Two parallel outbound appliers guarantee the next capability will drift the same way.

## What

- Collapse outbound application to **one** path. Either delete the dead typed leaf `_apply_outbound_update` (and its now-misleading tests), or make it the single production path and route the batch dispatch through it — chosen so there is no separately-tested parallel that production skips.
- Retarget the existing typed-leaf tests at the surviving production path.
- Add **one** test that drives a multi-sub-op outbound UPDATE — scalar fields + comments + labels + links + parent together — through the production path in a single mutation (the sub-ops were only ever tested in isolation, in separate files).

## Scope

- In: applier consolidation in `apply_outbound.py` / `batch_dispatch.py` / `applier.py`; the combined multi-sub-op test.
- Out: sub-op telemetry (story E).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Exactly one outbound applier path remains in production; the retired path is deleted (no dead, separately-tested applier code).
- [ ] A single test drives fields + comments + labels + links + parent through the production path in one UPDATE and asserts each sub-op took effect.
- [ ] No capability regresses (parent, comments, labels, links still apply); full reconciler suite green.
- [ ] The combined test asserts effects (sub-op outcomes), not interaction/mock-call-counts.
data.title: Collapse the dual outbound applier paths to one production path
